title: gagea bracteata
intro: Gagea bracteata, also known as the small-flowered adder's tongue or wood lily, is a species of flowering plant in the lily family that is native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Gagea bracteata has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and improving digestion. Gagea bracteata is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and may be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
